overview: Splits a single XLIFF document into multiple documents, one per `<file>` element. All content outside `<file>` elements (e.g., `<header>`, processing instructions) is copied as-is into each split document. An XLIFF document with only one `<file>` element is written out unaltered. Use the **XLIFF Joiner Step** to reassemble split files.

bigFile.help:
Uses a **stream-based (SAX) parser** instead of the default DOM-based parser. This allows processing very large XLIFF files (e.g., several hundreds of MB) that would otherwise exceed available memory.

When disabled, a DOM-based parser is used, which loads the entire document into memory.
notes: When using stream-based processing, any XML content **between** `<file>` elements is ignored and not copied into the output files.

fileMarker.help:
The marker string appended to the base filename of each generated output file, placed just before the numeric counter.

For example, with marker `_PART` and input `document.xlf`, outputs are named `document_PART1.xlf`, `document_PART2.xlf`, etc.

updateSDLTranslationStatus.help:
Updates the `translation_type` and `translation_status` flags in `<iws:status>` elements of the output documents. This is specific to **WorldServer XLIFF** documents.

Also **removes** the `target_content` attribute from `<iws:status>` elements.
notes: This option is specific to SDL WorldServer XLIFF documents and has no effect on standard XLIFF files.

translationTypeValue.help: The value written into the `translation_type` attribute of `<iws:status>` elements in the output documents.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this value to be applied

translationStatusValue.help: The value written into the `translation_status` attribute of `<iws:status>` elements in the output documents.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this value to be applied

restoreOriginalNames.help: Restores the original file names stored in the XLIFF `<file>` element's `original` attribute, instead of using the generated numbered naming convention.

limitations: When using the **Process big file** (stream-based) option, XML content between `<file>` elements is ignored and not copied into the output files.
An XLIFF document with only one `<file>` element is written out unaltered (no splitting occurs).
processingNotes: The step outputs multiple numbered XLIFF documents to the output path.
The received input event is passed through unaltered — the split files are written as side-output.
Content outside `<file>` elements (e.g., `<header>`, XML declarations) is duplicated into each output file when using the default DOM parser.
